explanation: PEG-240/HDI copolymer bis-decyltetradeceth-20 ether is a complex PEG-modified polyurethane and fatty alcohol copolymer used as an associative thickener and rheology modifier in water-based skincare formulas. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations, functioning as a texture and stability modifier.

PEG-2M (high-molecular-weight polyethylene glycol) is used as a thickener and viscosity modifier in skincare formulas. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical use concentrations, functioning as a texture modifier rather than a skin-active ingredient.

PEG-30 dipolyhydroxystearate is a PEG-modified polyhydroxystearic acid ester used as an emulsifier in water-in-oil and sunscreen formulas, where it helps disperse mineral UV filters like zinc oxide and titanium dioxide for a more elegant, non-chalky texture. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG-30 stearate is a PEG-modified stearate emulsifier used in skincare formulas to create stable oil-in-water emulsions with a smooth texture. It is generally well-tolerated; individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG-4 (low-molecular-weight polyethylene glycol) is used as a mild humectant and solubilizer in skincare formulas. It is generally well-tolerated, though its low molecular weight makes it a stronger skin penetration enhancer than higher-molecular-weight PEG variants; individuals with PEG sensitivity should approach with caution.

PEG-4 dilaurate is a PEG-modified dilauric acid ester used as a mild emulsifier and solubilizer in skincare and cleansing formulas. It is generally well-tolerated at typical use concentrations, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG-4 laurate is a PEG-modified lauric acid ester used as a mild emulsifier and solubilizer in skincare formulas. It is generally well-tolerated at typical use concentrations, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG-40 hydrogenated castor oil is a PEG-modified hydrogenated castor oil widely used as a gentle emulsifier and solubilizer for fragrances, essential oils, and oil-soluble actives in water-based formulas. It is considered one of the milder PEG-based emulsifiers and is generally well-tolerated; individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the modification.

PEG-40 stearate is a PEG-modified stearate emulsifier used in skincare formulas to create stable oil-in-water emulsions. It is generally well-tolerated; individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG-50 shea butter is a PEG-modified shea butter derivative that delivers shea butter's conditioning fatty acid content in a water-soluble form that is easier to incorporate into water-based formulas. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG-6 caprylic/capric glycerides is a PEG-modified glyceride emollient and emulsifier derived from coconut oil fatty acids, used to improve the spreadability and skin feel of emulsions while providing mild conditioning. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG-60 almond glycerides is a high-ethoxylation PEG-modified almond oil glyceride used as a gentle emulsifier and solubilizer in skincare formulas. Its high ethoxylation number makes it a milder choice; individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG-60 hydrogenated castor oil is a high-ethoxylation PEG-modified castor oil used as a gentle emulsifier and solubilizer for fragrances and oil-soluble actives in water-based formulas. Its higher ethoxylation number makes it milder than lower PEG variants; individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the modification.

PEG-7 (polyethylene glycol 350) is a low-to-moderate molecular weight PEG polymer used as a mild humectant and solubilizer in skincare formulas. It is generally well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use concentrations; individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol base.

PEG-7 glyceryl cocoate is a PEG-modified glyceryl cocoate used as a mild emulsifier, conditioning agent, and solubilizer in cleansers and lotions, providing skin-softening effects alongside emulsification. It is generally well-tolerated; individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG-75 shea butter glycerides is a high-ethoxylation PEG-modified shea butter glyceride used as a gentle emollient, emulsifier, and conditioning agent in skincare formulas. Its high ethoxylation number makes it a milder emulsifier choice; individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG-9 (polyethylene glycol 400) is a moderate molecular weight PEG polymer used as a mild humectant, solubilizer, and skin-conditioning agent in skincare formulas. It is generally well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use concentrations; individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol base.

PEG-9 polydimethylsiloxyethyl dimethicone is a PEG-silicone copolymer emulsifier used in water-in-silicone formulas, enabling stable combination of water and silicone phases with a smooth, lightweight skin feel. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG/PPG-17/6 copolymer is a block copolymer of polyethylene glycol and polypropylene glycol used as a mild emulsifier, solubilizer, and humectant in skincare formulas. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene/polypropylene glycol composition.

PEG/PPG-18/18 dimethicone is a PEG/PPG-modified silicone copolymer used as an emulsifier and texture modifier in water-in-silicone formulas, contributing a smooth, lightweight skin feel. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene/polypropylene glycol modification.

Pelargonium graveolens (rose geranium) flower oil is an essential oil rich in geraniol, linalool, and citronellol — multiple EU-regulated fragrance allergens — with documented contact sensitization risk. It can trigger allergic contact dermatitis and an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin, particularly in leave-on products.

Pentaerythrityl stearate/caprate/caprylate/adipate is a polyfunctional ester emollient providing a rich, non-greasy skin feel with excellent film-forming and spreadability properties in anhydrous formulas and lip products. It is well-tolerated and non-comedogenic.

Pentaerythrityl tetra-di-t-butyl hydroxyhydrocinnamate (Irganox 1010) is a synthetic hindered phenol antioxidant used in skincare formulas to prevent oxidative degradation of oils, emollients, and other formula components during storage. It is considered well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use concentrations and functions as a formula stabilizer rather than a skin-active ingredient.

Pentaerythrityl tetra-di-t-butyl hydroxyhydrocinnamate — the soft hyphens are a data artifact from ingredient label parsing — is a synthetic hindered phenol antioxidant used to prevent oxidative degradation of formula components during storage. It is considered well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use concentrations and functions as a formula stabilizer.

Pentaerythrityl tetra-dibutyl hydroxyhydrocinnamate is a variant name for the Irganox 1010-type synthetic hindered phenol antioxidant used to prevent oxidative degradation of oils and emollients in formula storage. It is considered well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use concentrations and functions as a formula stabilizer.

Pentaerythrityl tetraethylhexanoate is a polyfunctional branched-chain ester emollient providing a dry, non-greasy skin feel with excellent oxidative stability and spreadability. It is well-tolerated, non-comedogenic, and used in moisturizers and sunscreens as a comfortable, stable skin-feel enhancer.

Pentaerythrityl tetraisostearate is a polyfunctional ester emollient derived from isostearic acid providing a rich, non-greasy, long-lasting skin feel in anhydrous formulas and lip products. It is well-tolerated, non-comedogenic, and used for its luxurious texture and good adhesion in color cosmetic and sunscreen formulas.

Pentylene glycol is a mild diol solvent and humectant used as a preservative booster, moisturizer, and skin-feel enhancer that improves formula stability while drawing moisture to the skin. It is well-tolerated, low-sensitization, and has become a popular alternative to propylene glycol in clean and sensitive-skin formula systems.

Perfume is an alternate term for fragrance (parfum) — an undisclosed blend of scenting compounds that can legally contain dozens of sensitizing chemicals under a single label term without individually naming them. It is one of the leading categories of skincare contact allergens, and fragrance-free formulas are the safest choice for reactive or sensitive skin.

Perlite is a naturally occurring volcanic glass mineral used in cosmetics as an absorbent, mattifying agent, and texture enhancer in facial powders and foundations. It is considered inert, non-sensitizing, and well-tolerated, providing oil-absorbing and skin-smoothing properties similar to silica.
